title: Graphic and multimedia designers
description: Develop and implement websites, web applications, application databases, and interactive web interfaces. Evaluate code to ensure that it is properly structured, meets industry standards, and is compatible with browsers and devices. Optimize website performance, scalability, and server-side code and processes. May develop website infrastructure and integrate websites with other computer applications. Design digital user interfaces or websites. Develop and test layouts, interfaces, functionality, and navigation menus to ensure compatibility and usability across browsers or devices. May use web framework applications as well as client-side code and processes. May evaluate web design following web and accessibility standards, and may analyze web use metrics and optimize websites for marketability and search engine ranking. May design and test interfaces that facilitate the human-computer interaction and maximize the usability of digital devices, websites, and software with a focus on aesthetics and design. May create graphics used in websites and manage website content and links. Design core features of video games. Specify innovative game and role-play mechanics, story lines, and character biographies. Create and maintain design documentation. Guide and collaborate with production staff to produce games as designed. (Based on 16 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients to discuss and determine layout design, including designs, concepts, and sample layouts based on knowledge of layout principles and aesthetic design concepts. Create basic designs, drawings, and illustrations for product labels, cartons, direct mail, or television. Review final layouts and suggest improvements as needed. Prepare digital files for printing and maintain portfolios of artistic work to demonstrate styles, interests, and abilities.

Create designs and layouts for websites and multimedia campaigns, utilizing previous work products and conducting thorough research to explore the use of new materials, technologies, and products to incorporate into designs. Prepare illustrations or rough sketches of material, discussing them with clients or supervisors and making necessary changes. Integrate visual elements to produce desired effects such as the illustration of ideas, emotions, or moods, and create graphics and layouts for websites, product illustrations, and company logos. Produce still and animated graphics for various media, ensuring a focus on aesthetics and usability across devices and platforms.

Design and prepare layouts for various materials, ensuring they adhere to web and accessibility standards. Assess and analyze specifications, sketches, drawings, and related data to create user-friendly designs that comprise functionality and navigation. Draft working drawings for instructional purposes and ensure that completed works are ready for presentation. Use computer software to generate new images and manipulate designs to highlight key content.

Execute the design and development processes, including sketching, drawing, and using computer software to create innovative graphics and animations that meet client expectations. Collaborate with engineers and other technical experts when necessary to ensure design integrity and to integrate multimedia elements effectively. Keep abreast of trends, art exhibitions, and developments in the field to foster creativity and enhance design execution.

Utilize various materials and techniques to create artwork that visually communicates concepts and ideas, collaborating with other creatives to enhance project outcomes. Produce monochromatic and colored illustrations, utilizing hand drawing, digital mediums, and traditional approaches. Experiment with different artistic techniques to deliver high-quality outcomes that resonate with audiences.

Prepare technical documents and maintain configuration control systems for projects, ensuring compliance with design requirements. Provide detailed specifications for production, review completed works for accuracy, and assist in the assembly and presentation of final layouts. Participate in training sessions to remain updated on industry best practices and methodologies.
Remove relevant design tasks that do not align with the core responsibilities of graphic and multimedia designers.

Confer with production staff to resolve design issues, conduct research and adapt to client needs, and maintain organizational integrity throughout the design process. Supervise teams during installation and production phases to ensure alignment with design specifications and standards. Monitor project outcomes and help identify areas for improvement for future projects.

Collaborate with others to obtain products or other display items and consult with advertising or sales staff to determine type of merchandise to be featured. Develop ideas or plans for merchandise displays, and change or rotate window displays, interior display areas, or signage to reflect changes in inventory or promotions.

Plan commercial displays to entice and appeal to customers. Arrange properties, furniture, merchandise, backdrops, or other accessories according to prepared sketches and construct displays or display components using various materials and tools.

Take photographs of displays or signage and document design ideas in the form of sketches or drawings. Prepare previews of display layouts and review design specifications to ensure effective presentation.
Research health and safety code requirements to inform design, and confer with clients to determine factors affecting planning of interior environments, such as budget and architectural preferences.

Design and produce displays and materials for decorating windows and interior displays based on specified requirements. This includes planning for location-specific issues such as space limitations and safety concerns.

Coordinate with other professionals, such as contractors, architects, or engineers, to ensure successful implementation of design plans. Estimate costs and material requirements for proposed designs and present to clients for approval.

Maintain understanding of current design technologies or trends through continuing education, workshops, and other professional development activities. Review competitive products and evaluate and implement design improvements based on audience feedback and industry developments.

Generate comprehensive design documentation that includes specifications, construction plans, and layout details for exhibits or events. Conduct design reviews and collaborate with other team members throughout the design and implementation process.
Perform checks of installations or exhibits to ensure compliance with design specifications and quality standards. Review and revise layouts or designs as necessary to adhere to project goals.

Incorporate technical considerations into designs, taking into account budgets, performance requirements, and accessibility standards. Evaluate code and ensure projects are structured and compatible across devices and platforms.

Design, create, and maintain multimedia projects, including graphics, animations, and interactive elements using various digital tools and technologies. This includes creating mock-ups, identifying user needs, and iterating designs based on feedback.

Create gameplay prototypes and design core game features such as mechanics and character biographies. Collaborate with production staff to ensure proper game development and adherence to the original design vision.

Prepare and revise initial sketches using graphical design software, and achieve an appropriate visual style through collaboration with artists. Document all aspects of game design through layouts and flowcharts.
Provide feedback to production staff regarding technical issues or adherence to design. Engage in the quality assurance process by preparing test plans and reviewing gameplay experiences.
Maintain, adjust, and clean equipment used in design processes, ensuring high standards of production quality.

Provide descriptions of web site specifications, such as product features, programming languages, and layouts for digital interfaces and publications. Develop or update design documentation as required for clarity in execution.
Respond to user inquiries and interact with users to gather feedback that may influence design improvements and updates. Implement procedures for ongoing revision and enhancements of designs.

Collaborate with multiple stakeholders to define requirements for digital features and elements. Conduct regular reviews to assess the effectiveness of design decisions and strategies in meeting the project's goals.

Incorporate technical considerations into web site design plans, including budgets, equipment, performance requirements, and legal issues such as accessibility and privacy. Document technical factors such as server load, bandwidth, database performance, and browser and device types. Develop, validate, and document test routines and schedules to ensure that test cases mimic external interfaces and address all browser and device types. Identify problems uncovered by testing or customer feedback, and correct or refer them to appropriate personnel for correction. Develop new visual design concepts and modify them based on stakeholder feedback. Develop style guidelines for web site content and create prototypes or models of web designs, including physical, interface, logical, or data models. Design, build, or maintain web sites using authoring or scripting languages, content creation tools, and digital media. Evaluate web design following web and accessibility standards and analyze metrics to optimize websites.
